channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Amazing Global Women in Management Leadership Program Gwim 2026
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Empowering Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is a prestigious international leadership initiative designed to strengthen the leadership capacity of women professionals and entrepreneurs across the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with support from the ExxonMobil Foundation, the program aims to accelerate women’s economic leadership and empower participants with the […]

The post Amazing Global Women in Management Leadership Program Gwim 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Empowering Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 (GWIM 76) is a prestigious international leadership initiative designed to strengthen the leadership capacity of women professionals and entrepreneurs across the world. Implemented by Counterpart International with support from the ExxonMobil Foundation, the program aims to accelerate women’s economic leadership and empower participants with the skills needed to drive inclusive economic and social transformation in their communities and organizations.

The Global Women in Management Program (GWIM) was launched in 2005 and has since become one of the most influential leadership development initiatives supporting women leaders worldwide. Through integrated training, mentorship, executive coaching, and global networking opportunities, the program equips participants with the tools needed to expand their leadership influence and strengthen the institutions and enterprises they represent.

The 2026 cohort, known as GWIM 76, offers a hybrid learning model combining a two-week immersive leadership experience in Washington, DC with several months of structured virtual training and mentorship. This approach allows participants to engage deeply with leadership concepts while applying newly acquired skills in real-world professional environments.

Applications for the program close on April 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST.

Leadership Development Through the GWIM Program

The GWIM Leadership Program focuses on strengthening women’s leadership and management capacity while addressing systemic barriers that limit women’s economic advancement. The program emphasizes a results-oriented approach that combines academic knowledge, practical leadership training, and collaborative learning.

Participants will gain insights into leadership development, economic empowerment strategies, organizational management, and the role of innovation in modern leadership environments. The program also emphasizes how emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and digital platforms can support economic growth and institutional development.

By combining traditional leadership training with modern digital innovation strategies, the program prepares women leaders to navigate rapidly evolving economic and technological environments.

Two-Week Leadership Experience in Washington, DC

A central component of the program is the two-week in-person leadership training in Washington, DC, scheduled from October 19 to October 30, 2026.

During this immersive experience, participants will engage with policymakers, global development experts, academics, and thought leaders. The sessions will include leadership workshops, policy dialogues, collaborative discussions, and strategic leadership exercises designed to strengthen participants’ leadership capabilities.

The Washington, DC residency provides participants with valuable exposure to international policy environments and leadership practices. Participants will have the opportunity to learn from experienced professionals who are actively shaping economic and social development initiatives worldwide.

This in-person training experience also provides a collaborative environment where participants can exchange ideas, build professional networks, and develop partnerships that extend beyond the duration of the program.

Virtual Leadership Learning and Mentorship

After completing the Washington, DC training, participants will continue the program through six months of virtual learning, mentorship, and leadership development activities.

These virtual sessions will be conducted on a monthly or bi-monthly basis and will total approximately 16 to 20 hours of structured learning. The online component is designed to reinforce the leadership skills developed during the in-person training while providing ongoing mentorship and peer collaboration opportunities.

Participants will engage in discussions, leadership exercises, group work, and practical assignments that help them apply new knowledge within their organizations and communities.

The program also includes a practicum component, where participants develop action plans for their leadership and professional development. These plans enable participants to translate the lessons learned during the program into measurable outcomes in their professional environments.

Focus Areas of the Leadership Program

The GWIM program focuses on several key areas that are essential for effective leadership in the modern global economy.

Economic Leadership

Participants will develop the skills required to influence economic growth and development within their organizations and communities. Training sessions will focus on strategic planning, resource mobilization, and sustainable organizational growth.

Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation

The program integrates emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and digital platforms into leadership training. Participants will explore how digital transformation can support innovation, productivity, and organizational development.

Executive Leadership and Strategic Decision-Making

The program strengthens participants’ ability to lead organizations, manage complex challenges, and make strategic decisions. Leadership workshops and coaching sessions help participants develop the confidence and skills needed to lead effectively in dynamic environments.

Global Networking Opportunities

One of the most valuable aspects of the GWIM program is the opportunity to join a global network of women leaders. Participants will engage with women professionals from different regions and sectors, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and long-term partnerships.

The program fosters meaningful connections among participants and provides access to a global community of GWIM alumnae who continue to support each other’s leadership journeys.

Participating Countries

The 2026 cohort welcomes women leaders from the following countries:

Brazil, Egypt, Guyana, India, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Mexico, Mozambique, China, Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Vietnam, and the United Arab Emirates.

By bringing together women from diverse regions, the program promotes cross-cultural learning and global collaboration.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Empowering Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 (GWIM 76) and submit your application, click here:

Conclusion

The Global Women in Management Leadership Program 2026 offers a unique opportunity for women leaders to strengthen their leadership capacity, expand their professional networks, and gain practical skills in economic leadership and digital transformation. Through its hybrid learning model combining in-person training and virtual mentorship, the program equips participants with the knowledge and tools needed to lead transformational change within their organizations and communities.

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

 

The post Amazing Global Women in Management Leadership Program Gwim 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Unlock Global Grants for Africa: Free Funding Opportunities, Networking, and Program Support for Organizations
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Introduction: A Gateway to Global Opportunities for Africa Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa (GGA) is an inclusive and far-reaching platform designed to connect African individuals and institutions with credible, high-impact opportunities from across the world. The platform serves a diverse audience, including Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs), companies, universities, enterprises, startups, scholars, […]

The post Unlock Global Grants for Africa: Free Funding Opportunities, Networking, and Program Support for Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Introduction: A Gateway to Global Opportunities for Africa

Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa (GGA) is an inclusive and far-reaching platform designed to connect African individuals and institutions with credible, high-impact opportunities from across the world. The platform serves a diverse audience, including Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs), companies, universities, enterprises, startups, scholars, and other development-focused ventures.

At its core, GGA functions as a centralized hub where thousands of global funding opportunities, calls for proposals, fellowships, and partnerships are curated and shared. Its primary objective is to ensure that African stakeholders—regardless of size or stage—have access to timely, relevant, and actionable opportunities that align with their missions and goals.

All services provided by the platform are completely free of charge. Supported by a team of experienced experts, GGA not only aggregates opportunities but also ensures that users are consistently updated through its communication channels. By following the platform, users gain access to daily updates on newly available grants and programs tailored to various sectors and interests.

Commitment to Safety and Transparency

One of the defining features of the Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa platform is its strong commitment to protecting users from fraudulent schemes. In a space where grant scams and misleading opportunities are increasingly common, GGA takes deliberate steps to screen and verify all shared content before publication.

However, the platform also emphasizes the importance of personal due diligence. Users are strongly advised to independently assess every opportunity they pursue. A key warning sign highlighted by GGA is any application process that requests payment for services. Legitimate grant opportunities do not require applicants to pay fees during the application stage, and users are encouraged to avoid such engagements entirely.

This dual approach—platform-level screening combined with user-level vigilance—ensures a safer and more trustworthy experience for all participants.

Key Advantages of Joining the Platform

Becoming part of the Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa network offers several important benefits:

1. Timely Access to Grants and Calls for Proposals

GGA provides real-time updates on newly released grants and funding calls. These opportunities target a wide range of beneficiaries, including African and international CSOs, NGOs, enterprises, and companies. By staying connected to the platform, users can respond quickly to funding opportunities, increasing their chances of success.

2. Networking Opportunities

The platform facilitates connections among African scholars, universities, NGOs, and other institutions. When announcements are made, users can engage in collaborative opportunities that extend beyond borders. This networking aspect is particularly valuable for organizations seeking partnerships, knowledge exchange, and joint funding applications.

3. Program Consultation for Growing Ventures

In addition to sharing opportunities, GGA offers consultation support for emerging and growing initiatives across Africa. This includes guidance on program development, proposal writing, and strategic positioning. Such technical support is critical for organizations aiming to build resilience and scale their impact.

The Driving Philosophy: Addressing Africa’s Development Challenges

The foundation of GGA’s work is rooted in the recognition of Africa’s pressing development challenges. With approximately 226.7 million people facing hunger across the continent, there is an urgent need for coordinated action and sustainable solutions.

Charities, diaspora communities, NGOs, and companies continue to play a vital role in addressing these challenges. However, many of these actors face limitations in accessing funding, technical expertise, and global networks.

GGA seeks to bridge this gap by connecting, strengthening, and empowering a dynamic network of civil society organizations and other stakeholders. Its mission is to contribute to the eradication of poverty, inequality, and injustice by ensuring that those working on the frontlines have the resources and support they need.

Vision and Long-Term Impact

The platform has an ambitious vision: to engage more than 50,000 like-minded institutions both within and outside Africa. Through this network, GGA aims to indirectly impact up to 50 million people in need.

This vision underscores the platform’s belief in collective action. By enabling collaboration and resource mobilization at scale, GGA aspires to create a ripple effect where local initiatives grow into transformative movements with global reach.

Scope of Opportunities Available

GGA aggregates opportunities for a wide spectrum of stakeholders, including:

  • Individuals seeking scholarships, fellowships, or research funding

  • CSOs and NGOs pursuing grants for community projects

  • Startups and enterprises looking for investment and innovation funding

  • Large organizations and institutions aiming to expand their programs

Most of the grants shared on the platform are designed to strengthen organizations at the grassroots level. These grants focus on enhancing the capacity of local actors to mobilize resources, implement effective programs, and serve their communities more efficiently.

Some funding opportunities may also have specific geographic or thematic priorities, allowing organizations to align their applications with targeted development goals.

Demonstrated Impact: Grantmaking Achievements

The effectiveness of the platform and its broader ecosystem can be seen through recent grantmaking data. In the 2023/2024 financial year:

  • A total of 73 grants were awarded

  • 63 community philanthropy organizations benefited

  • 4 individual recipients received support

  • Grants were distributed across 30 countries

  • The total funding disbursed amounted to £1,419,803

These figures highlight the scale and reach of the funding landscape that GGA helps its users access. They also demonstrate the growing importance of community philanthropy as a mechanism for sustainable development.

Grant Eligibility: Who Can Apply?

The platform features opportunities that are particularly relevant to organizations engaged in community philanthropy. Eligible entities may include:

  • Community philanthropy organizations

  • Community foundations

  • Women’s funds

  • Environmental funds

  • National public foundations

  • Grassroots grantmakers

  • Any organization aligned with the principles of community philanthropy

These organizations are typically characterized by their strong connection to the communities they serve and their commitment to locally driven development.

Key Areas of Interest for Applicants

Organizations interested in applying for grants featured on the platform are often those that:

  • Aim to shift power and decision-making closer to communities

  • Focus on recognizing and strengthening local assets and resources

  • Experiment with innovative governance and resource mobilization approaches

  • Promote community-led development and participation

Additionally, applicants are expected to demonstrate a deep understanding of their communities, viewing them as sources of knowledge, relationships, and agency.

Organizational Practices and Values

Successful applicants typically exhibit the following practices:

  • Use grantmaking as a strategic tool to empower local groups

  • Foster a culture of giving and co-investment within their communities

  • Build trust, networks, and collaborative relationships

  • Address complex or underrepresented issues, even when they are unpopular

These values align with the broader goal of creating inclusive, participatory, and sustainable development systems.

Geographic Focus

While the platform serves a global audience, there is a strong emphasis on organizations based in:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • The Middle East

  • Central and Eastern Europe

  • Latin America and the Caribbean

Priority is often given to regions with limited access to funding opportunities. However, organizations in the Global North that represent disadvantaged communities may also be considered on a case-by-case basis, depending on funding restrictions.

Determining Your Fit

If your organization meets most of the criteria outlined above, it is likely a strong candidate for the types of opportunities shared through the platform. Even if you are unsure, exploring additional resources—such as educational materials on community philanthropy—can help you better understand your alignment and readiness.

Click Here on details on how to apply.

Conclusion

Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa stands as a vital resource for anyone seeking to access funding, build partnerships, and strengthen development initiatives across the continent. By offering free, curated, and verified opportunities, along with technical support and networking avenues, the platform empowers African stakeholders to take meaningful action.

Through its commitment to transparency, inclusivity, and impact, GGA is not only connecting people to opportunities but also contributing to a broader movement aimed at transforming communities and improving lives at scale.

Discover more similar grant opportunities Here.

The post Unlock Global Grants for Africa: Free Funding Opportunities, Networking, and Program Support for Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026: $2.5M+ Grants to Strengthen Youth Mental Health Workforce (Up to $500,000)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Rare Impact Fund, founded by Selena Gomez, has launched a major Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026 to strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce in the United States. This funding opportunity will provide $250,000 to $500,000 in grants to nonprofit organizations working to expand access to youth mental health support, workforce development, and community-based […]

The post Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026: $2.5M+ Grants to Strengthen Youth Mental Health Workforce (Up to $500,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Rare Impact Fund, founded by Selena Gomez, has launched a major Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026 to strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce in the United States.

This funding opportunity will provide $250,000 to $500,000 in grants to nonprofit organizations working to expand access to youth mental health support, workforce development, and community-based care systems.

If your organization is focused on youth mental health, workforce development, or community-based support systems, this is a significant funding opportunity to scale your impact.

About the Rare Impact Fund

The Rare Impact Fund was established to address the global youth mental health crisis by increasing access to:

  • Mental health care

  • Stigma-free community support

  • Culturally responsive resources

The Fund is mobilizing $100 million and currently supports 30 nonprofit partners across five continents, focusing on:

  • School-based mental health solutions

  • Networks of support

  • Suicide prevention and crisis response

The initiative is fiscally sponsored by the Hopewell Fund, a U.S.-based public charity supporting innovative social change projects.

What Is the 2026 Funding Opportunity?

The Rare Impact Fund is investing $2.5 million+ in organizations that strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce.

Grant Details

  • Funding Amount: $250,000 – $500,000

  • Grant Duration: 2 years

  • Total Funding Pool: $2.5M+

  • Number of Grants: At least 5 organizations

Funded projects should focus on building a sustainable pipeline of nonclinical mental health providers supporting young people.

Who Are Nonclinical Mental Health Providers?

Nonclinical providers are individuals who support youth mental health without providing formal clinical diagnosis or treatment.

They play a critical role in expanding access to care through community-based, culturally relevant, and stigma-free support.

Examples include:

  • Community Health Workers

  • Peer Support Specialists

  • Care Navigators

  • Health Educators

These professionals:

  • Provide guidance, mentorship, and emotional support

  • Help youth navigate services

  • Deliver prevention and education programmes

  • Extend the reach of clinical systems

Why This Funding Matters

The youth mental health crisis is growing rapidly:

  • Over 12 million young people experience mental health disorders annually

  • Most lack access to adequate care

  • 93% of behavioral health workers report burnout

There is a critical shortage of licensed clinicians, making nonclinical providers essential to bridging the gap.

However, these roles are often:

  • Undervalued

  • Underpaid

  • Lacking clear career pathways

This initiative aims to professionalize and strengthen this workforce while improving outcomes for young people.

What Projects Will Be Funded?

The Rare Impact Fund is looking for projects that strengthen the full workforce pipeline, including:

1. Attraction and Recruitment

  • Bringing diverse individuals into mental health roles

2. Development and Training

  • Building skills and competencies

3. Placement and Entry

  • Supporting transition into real roles

4. Upskilling and Career Growth

  • Providing advancement opportunities

5. Retention and Sustainability

  • Ensuring long-term workforce stability

Successful projects should be:

  • Community-rooted

  • Evidence-based

  • Youth-centered

  • Focused on workforce development (not direct service delivery alone)

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, organizations must:

  • Be a registered nonprofit (501(c)(3) or equivalent)

  • Operate in the United States

  • Focus program activities within the U.S.

  • Be non-political

  • Submit only one application

  • Request between $250,000 and $500,000

Organizations must also commit to:

  • Annual cohort meetings

  • Learning sessions (quarterly)

  • Reporting and storytelling activities

Priority Considerations

Preference will be given to organizations that demonstrate:

  • Experience supporting nonclinical mental health providers

  • Strong focus on youth populations

  • Workforce development expertise

  • Integration of youth voice and lived experience

  • Clear equity strategies targeting underserved communities

Application Process

The application follows a two-stage process:

Stage 1: Letter of Intent (LOI)

Applicants must submit:

  • 1–2 page narrative

  • Organizational background and experience

  • Proposed program overview

  • Workforce gap addressed

  • Youth focus and equity strategy

Apply here: https://forms.gle/jeP6kpsLMt6haw9m8

Stage 2: Full Proposal (By Invitation Only)

Top 60 applicants will be invited to submit a detailed proposal including:

  • Program design

  • Theory of change

  • Budget and sustainability plan

  • Due diligence documentation

Key Dates

  • LOI Opens: March 9, 2026

  • Webinar: March 18, 2026

  • LOI Deadline: April 10, 2026

  • Full Proposal Invitations: May 11, 2026

  • Full Proposal Deadline: June 12, 2026

  • Grant Awards Announced: August 2026

  • Funding Distributed: September–October 2026

Informational Webinar

An applicant webinar will be held to explain:

  • Program goals

  • Eligibility requirements

  • Application process

Organizations must register in advance to attend. A recording will be shared afterward.

What Success Looks Like

The initiative aims to achieve:

  • Stronger career pathways for nonclinical providers

  • Improved workforce retention

  • Increased recognition of nonclinical roles

  • Better youth mental health outcomes

For the first time, the Rare Impact Fund is launching a public Request for Proposals, allocating more than $2.5 million in total – including a $500,000 investment from the Enlight Foundation – to strengthen the nonclinical youth mental health workforce. This new grant program will support nonprofits across the workforce talent pipeline, from recruitment and training to retention, with a focus on roles that support young people outside traditional clinical settings. 

This is a high-impact funding opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of:

  • Youth development

  • Mental health

  • Workforce systems

By strengthening the nonclinical workforce, this initiative has the potential to transform how young people access mental health support at scale.

APPLY NOW 

Explore More Opportunities

For more global grants, fellowships, and funding opportunities:

Visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=grant

The post Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026: $2.5M+ Grants to Strengthen Youth Mental Health Workforce (Up to $500,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector 2026: Funding and Investor Access for Climate-Smart Agri-SMEs in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Impact-driven agribusinesses in sub-Saharan Africa now have a powerful opportunity to access technical support, investor networks, and potential capital through the CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector 2026. Organized through a partnership between ClimateShot Investor Coalition (CLIC) and FINCA International, the program is designed to support innovative agricultural SMEs that are scaling climate- and nature-positive solutions. The […]

The post CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector 2026: Funding and Investor Access for Climate-Smart Agri-SMEs in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Impact-driven agribusinesses in sub-Saharan Africa now have a powerful opportunity to access technical support, investor networks, and potential capital through the CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector 2026. Organized through a partnership between ClimateShot Investor Coalition (CLIC) and FINCA International, the program is designed to support innovative agricultural SMEs that are scaling climate- and nature-positive solutions.

The initiative is part of a broader effort to accelerate sustainable agriculture and food systems across Africa by helping businesses demonstrate measurable impact and connect with investors looking for high-impact opportunities.

What Is the CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector?

The CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector is a free support program that helps agrifood SMEs prepare for investment by strengthening their ability to communicate climate, nature, and biodiversity impact to potential investors.

Through this program, selected companies receive expert technical assistance and gain exposure to global investors who are actively seeking climate-focused investment opportunities in emerging markets.

This program also connects with the FINCA Ventures Prize Competition, allowing applicants to pursue multiple opportunities through a single application process.

Key Benefits for Selected Companies

Selected agrifood SMEs will gain access to several high-value opportunities, including:

  • Technical Assistance from Industry Experts
    Work directly with specialists from the Connector program to refine your investment narrative and demonstrate climate and biodiversity impact.

  • Investor Exposure and Capital Raising Support
    Receive guidance on how to position your business to attract commercial capital from impact investors.

  • Pitch Opportunity at the AFSF 2026 Deal Room
    Selected participants may pitch their companies to investors at the Africa Food Systems Forum (AFSF) 2026.

  • Eligibility for the FINCA Prize
    Companies applying under eligible categories may also compete for the FINCA Ventures Prize.

Program Focus Areas

The Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems category targets companies offering solutions that strengthen agrifood value chains while improving outcomes for farmers and the environment.

Examples of solutions include:

  • Access to sustainable agricultural inputs

  • Financing for smallholder farmers

  • Agricultural knowledge and extension services

  • Irrigation and mechanization technologies

  • Climate and weather information services

  • Insurance products for farmers

  • Digital agricultural tools

  • Post-harvest management systems

  • Market access platforms

  • Offtake and supply chain solutions

Innovative solutions addressing these areas are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Entrepreneurs must create an account on the application portal and complete the online form. Applicants can download sample applications to prepare their responses before submission.

Applications do not need to be completed in a single session. Candidates can save progress and return to finalize their submission before the deadline.

Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (Midnight GMT-4)

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Information Session and Q&A

Applicants interested in learning more about the program can attend a live information session hosted by the program organizers.

Date: March 27, 2026
Time: 12:00 PM GMT
Platform: Zoom.

REGISTER HERE. 

During this session, the organizers will walk through the application process and answer questions from prospective applicants.

Support and Contact Information

Applicants who need additional support can reach out to the program teams directly.

For questions about the CLIC Connector Program, contact: clic@cpiglobal.org

For questions about the FINCA Ventures Prize, contact: melissa.tickle@finca.org

Entrepreneurs working to build climate-resilient agriculture and sustainable food systems across Africa are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this opportunity to connect with investors and scale their impact.

The post CLIC Agrifood Investment Connector 2026: Funding and Investor Access for Climate-Smart Agri-SMEs in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Justice Mission (IJM) Internships & Fellowships 2026: Paid, Remote & Worldwide Opportunities
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The International Justice Mission (IJM) Internships and Fellowships 2026 offer a unique opportunity for students, graduates, and professionals to gain hands-on experience in global justice and human rights. These programmes are available worldwide, including remote options, and provide a monthly stipend to support participants during their placement. Whether you are looking to build your career […]

The post International Justice Mission (IJM) Internships & Fellowships 2026: Paid, Remote & Worldwide Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Justice Mission (IJM) Internships and Fellowships 2026 offer a unique opportunity for students, graduates, and professionals to gain hands-on experience in global justice and human rights. These programmes are available worldwide, including remote options, and provide a monthly stipend to support participants during their placement.

Whether you are looking to build your career in international development, law, advocacy, or social impact, IJM provides a platform to apply your skills while contributing to a safer and more just world.

Key Details

  • Host Organization: International Justice Mission (IJM)

  • Location: Worldwide (including remote, hybrid, and in-person roles)

  • Work Mode: Remote, part-time, full-time, and in-person options available

  • Funding: Monthly stipend provided

  • Duration:

    • 2–3 months (short-term internships)

    • 6–12 months (fellowships and specialized roles)

    • 12 months (global internships and fellowships)

  • Eligibility: Students, graduates, and experienced professionals

  • Application Timeline: Varies by intake cycle

  • Availability: Open to applicants worldwide

About the Programme

IJM is a global organization committed to protecting vulnerable communities from violence and exploitation. Through its internship and fellowship programmes, IJM partners with individuals from diverse backgrounds to strengthen justice systems and support community-based solutions.

Participants contribute to real projects across different departments and country offices, gaining exposure to international development, legal systems, policy work, and advocacy.

Programme Structure

IJM offers two main pathways for internships and fellowships:

D.C.-Based and Remote Internships and Fellowships

  • Duration: 2–3 months

  • Open to: Students who have completed at least two years of university

  • Legal Fellowship: 6–12 months (for candidates with a Juris Doctor degree)

  • Work Mode: Includes remote and flexible work arrangements

Application cycles:

  • Spring (January – April): Applications open in June

  • Summer (May – August): Applications open in October

  • Fall (September – December): Applications open in March

Global Internships and Fellowships

  • Duration: 12 months

  • Open to: Bachelor’s degree holders and experienced professionals

  • Location: IJM field offices worldwide

  • Deployment cycles: January and June each year

  • Application window: Opens 11–12 months before deployment

Funding and Benefits

Participants in IJM internships and fellowships receive support and development opportunities, including:

  • Monthly stipend to help cover living expenses

  • Professional mentorship from experienced practitioners

  • Exposure to international justice and development work

  • Opportunity to work on real-world projects with global teams

  • Access to a strong international professional network

  • Flexible remote and hybrid work options

Remote Opportunities

IJM provides remote internship and fellowship options for candidates who are unable to relocate. These roles may be available on a part-time or full-time basis and can offer flexible scheduling depending on academic or professional commitments.

Remote roles allow participants from anywhere in the world to contribute meaningfully to IJM’s mission while gaining valuable global experience.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following general requirements:

  • Must be at least 18 years old

  • Students must have completed a minimum of two years of university study (for short-term roles)

  • Bachelor’s degree required for global internships and fellowships

  • Cross-cultural experience is an advantage, especially for international placements

  • Strong interest in justice, human rights, or international development

  • Open to applicants from all countries, subject to local labor laws

Application Requirements

Candidates may be required to submit the following:

  • Updated CV or resume

  • Cover letter outlining motivation and relevant experience

  • Statement of faith (describing personal faith and its relevance to the role)

  • Academic or professional references

Applicants are encouraged to clearly demonstrate how their skills, experience, and values align with IJM’s mission and work.

Why Apply

The IJM Internship and Fellowship Programme offers more than just professional experience. It provides an opportunity to:

  • Contribute to global efforts to combat injustice and exploitation

  • Develop practical skills in an international work environment

  • Build a career in human rights, law, or development

  • Gain mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals

  • Work with a diverse, mission-driven global team

How to Apply

To explore current openings and submit your application, visit the official IJM internships page:

https://www.ijm.org/get-involved/interns-fellows

Explore More Opportunities

For more internships, fellowships, and global opportunities, visit:

https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=internship

IJM Internships and Fellowships 2026 provide a valuable pathway for individuals seeking to combine professional growth with meaningful global impact. With remote options, a monthly stipend, and worldwide availability, this programme is accessible to a wide range of applicants committed to building a more just and equitable world.

The post International Justice Mission (IJM) Internships & Fellowships 2026: Paid, Remote & Worldwide Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: James Aryam Postgraduate Scholarship 2026 for Female Refugees in Kenya and Uganda (Fully Funded Master’s Opportunities in Uganda)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The James Aryam Postgraduate Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering fully funded postgraduate study opportunities for eligible female refugees residing in Kenya or Uganda. This initiative is implemented in collaboration with Windle International Kenya and Windle International Uganda, providing talented refugee women the opportunity to pursue postgraduate education in Uganda. The scholarship aims […]

The post James Aryam Postgraduate Scholarship 2026 for Female Refugees in Kenya and Uganda (Fully Funded Master’s Opportunities in Uganda) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The James Aryam Postgraduate Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering fully funded postgraduate study opportunities for eligible female refugees residing in Kenya or Uganda. This initiative is implemented in collaboration with Windle International Kenya and Windle International Uganda, providing talented refugee women the opportunity to pursue postgraduate education in Uganda.

The scholarship aims to empower women affected by displacement and conflict by enabling them to access advanced education in education-related fields. Through financial support, academic opportunities, and mentorship, the program seeks to build a new generation of leaders who can contribute to rebuilding and strengthening their communities.

What the Scholarship Covers

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support for their postgraduate studies. The scholarship package includes:

  • Full tuition fees for the entire postgraduate programme

  • Monthly maintenance stipend to support living expenses

  • Relocation costs where applicable

  • Academic and professional support throughout the programme

This support ensures that scholars can fully focus on their studies without financial barriers.

Fields of Study

Scholarships are available for students pursuing taught Master’s degrees and Postgraduate Diplomas in education-related subjects. These programmes aim to develop skilled educators, policy experts, and leaders who can contribute to improving education systems in their communities.

Participating Universities

Applicants must apply to one or more of the following universities in Uganda:

  • Makerere University

  • Bugema University

  • Uganda Martyrs University

  • Uganda Christian University

  • University of Kisubi

Windle Trust International recommends that candidates apply to at least two universities to increase their chances of admission.

Application Process

To apply for the scholarship, candidates must complete the following steps:

  1. Review the full eligibility criteria to ensure that all requirements are met. Exceptions are not made to these criteria.

  2. Apply to at least one of the specified universities for August 2026 admissions in an eligible education-related programme.

  3. Complete the Windle Trust International scholarship application form via the official online portal.

  4. Upload proof of your university application submission when submitting the scholarship application.

  5. Attend an interview if shortlisted, which will take place either in the applicant’s current country of residence or online.

Applicants must complete both the university application and the scholarship application in order to be considered.

Important Dates

  • Applications open: 16 March 2026

  • Application deadline: 16 April 2026

  • Interview period: May 2026

Late applications will not be accepted, so candidates are strongly encouraged to begin their applications as early as possible.

Application Costs

Windle Trust International does not charge any fees for scholarship applications.

However, applicants should note that university application fees may apply, and candidates are responsible for covering these costs themselves.

Commitment to Equal Opportunity

The scholarship programme follows a strong policy of equal opportunity and non-discrimination. Applications are encouraged from individuals from diverse backgrounds, including refugees, minorities, persons with disabilities, and individuals affected by conflict.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should review the full eligibility criteria and submit their application through the official online portal:

Application link: jamesaryam.windle.org.uk

APPLY HERE. 

For questions or application guidance, candidates may contact: postgraduate@windle.org.uk

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

The post James Aryam Postgraduate Scholarship 2026 for Female Refugees in Kenya and Uganda (Fully Funded Master’s Opportunities in Uganda) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 Global DPI Research Grant by the Center for Financial Inclusion: Fully Funded Opportunity Offering Up to $50,000 for Researchers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The 2026 Call for Research Proposals by the Center for Financial Inclusion represents one of the most significant global funding opportunities for researchers working in financial inclusion, digital systems, and public infrastructure. This competitive research grant—offered under the Global DPI Insights Community—aims to generate practical, real-world evidence on how digital public infrastructure (DPI) systems are […]

The post 2026 Global DPI Research Grant by the Center for Financial Inclusion: Fully Funded Opportunity Offering Up to $50,000 for Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 Call for Research Proposals by the Center for Financial Inclusion represents one of the most significant global funding opportunities for researchers working in financial inclusion, digital systems, and public infrastructure. This competitive research grant—offered under the Global DPI Insights Community—aims to generate practical, real-world evidence on how digital public infrastructure (DPI) systems are designed, implemented, and sustained across different regions.

This opportunity is ideal for researchers, institutions, and collaborative teams seeking to influence global policy, strengthen financial systems, and contribute to inclusive economic development through high-impact research.

Overview of the Opportunity

The 2026 research grant is part of a broader global initiative launched in March 2026 to support rigorous, context-driven research on digital public infrastructure (DPI).

DPI refers to foundational digital systems—such as digital identity, payment platforms, and data-sharing frameworks—that enable inclusive financial services and economic participation.

This funding round is particularly focused on bridging the growing gap between rapid DPI expansion and the limited availability of evidence-based insights needed for long-term sustainability and governance.

Funding Value and Duration

  • Grant Amount: Up to USD $50,000 per project

  • Project Duration: Approximately 6 to 8 months

The funding is designed to cover:

  • Data collection and fieldwork

  • Research analysis

  • Stakeholder engagement

  • Dissemination of findings

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants)

This is a globally open opportunity, meaning applicants from any country or continent can apply.

Eligible Categories of Applicants:

  • Research institutions (universities, think tanks, policy research bodies)

  • Independent researchers

  • Collaborative research teams

  • Partnerships involving local stakeholders (strongly encouraged)

Ideal Applicants Include:

  • Academics and scholars in financial inclusion or digital systems

  • Policy researchers and analysts

  • Development practitioners

  • Interdisciplinary research teams

The program strongly encourages collaborations between global researchers and local experts, ensuring that research outputs are grounded in real-world contexts.

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to applicants worldwide

  • Projects must be anchored in specific countries or regions rather than purely global or theoretical studies

This emphasis ensures that findings are locally relevant and actionable, especially in emerging and developing economies.

Key Research Focus Areas

Applicants must align their proposals with the core themes of the grant, which include:

1. Design and Implementation of DPI Systems

  • How digital systems are structured and deployed

  • Technical and governance frameworks

2. Financial Sustainability

  • Long-term viability of DPI initiatives

  • Funding models and economic trade-offs

3. Policy and Operational Trade-offs

  • Balancing innovation with regulation

  • Managing risks, costs, and inclusivity

4. Inclusion and Market Dynamics

  • Consumer protection

  • Competition in digital financial ecosystems

  • Accessibility for underserved populations

5. Locally Grounded Evidence

  • Research must reflect real-world experiences

  • Country-specific insights are prioritized over broad generalizations

Why This Opportunity Matters

Digital public infrastructure is rapidly becoming the backbone of modern economies. However, as these systems expand globally, critical gaps remain in understanding how they function in diverse environments.

This grant directly addresses that gap by:

  • Supporting evidence-based policymaking

  • Strengthening inclusive financial systems

  • Encouraging cross-sector collaboration

  • Generating insights that influence global development agendas

According to program leaders, the challenge is no longer building DPI systems—but ensuring they are sustainable, secure, and inclusive.

Expected Outcomes of Funded Projects

Successful applicants will contribute to:

  • Policy recommendations for governments

  • Practical frameworks for DPI implementation

  • Research publications and global knowledge sharing

  • Improved financial inclusion strategies

Projects are expected to deliver actionable insights, not just theoretical findings.

Application Requirements

Although detailed submission guidelines are provided on the official page, a strong application typically includes:

  • A clearly defined research question

  • Methodology and data collection plan

  • Geographic focus (country or region-specific)

  • Evidence of collaboration (especially with local stakeholders)

  • Budget justification

  • Expected impact and dissemination strategy

Apply here

Important Tips for Applicants

To increase your chances of success:

  • Focus on real-world problems, not abstract theory

  • Demonstrate local partnerships or engagement

  • Align your research with policy relevance

  • Provide a clear implementation plan within 6–8 months

  • Highlight how your findings will influence decision-makers

Deadline

30th April 2026 (midnight EDT) 

The 2026 Center for Financial Inclusion Research Grant is a highly competitive and impactful funding opportunity for researchers committed to shaping the future of digital financial systems. With up to $50,000 in funding, global eligibility, and a strong emphasis on real-world impact, this grant offers a unique platform to contribute to inclusive, sustainable, and scalable digital economies.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post 2026 Global DPI Research Grant by the Center for Financial Inclusion: Fully Funded Opportunity Offering Up to $50,000 for Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Al Qasimi Foundation Doctoral Research Grants 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in the UAE
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Al Qasimi Foundation is inviting ambitious and high-achieving PhD students to apply for its Doctoral Research Grants, a unique opportunity for scholars to undertake policy-relevant research in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), with a special focus on Ras Al Khaimah. Designed to bridge academia and policymaking, these grants empower doctoral candidates to produce research […]

The post Al Qasimi Foundation Doctoral Research Grants 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in the UAE appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Al Qasimi Foundation is inviting ambitious and high-achieving PhD students to apply for its Doctoral Research Grants, a unique opportunity for scholars to undertake policy-relevant research in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), with a special focus on Ras Al Khaimah. Designed to bridge academia and policymaking, these grants empower doctoral candidates to produce research that can directly inform local and national policy while fostering professional and academic growth.

The grant fully funds the research phase of your doctoral dissertation, providing the necessary support to cover all field research-related expenses, including return airfare, furnished accommodation for up to 12 months, a stipend for living costs, and research support. Fellows are expected to produce one to two policy papers, deliver at least one presentation to the local research community, and may record a podcast to disseminate their findings.

The application deadline is April 1, 2026 (23:59 GST). Applicants should plan to submit their proposals through the Foundation’s online grant management portal before this date.

Purpose and Impact of the Doctoral Research Grants

The Al Qasimi Foundation aims to:

  • Support doctoral candidates conducting innovative, applied research that addresses real-world challenges in Ras Al Khaimah and the UAE

  • Build the UAE’s research capacity through high-quality, policy-relevant studies

  • Promote a community of scholars by fostering collaboration among international universities and local institutions

  • Encourage cross-cultural exchange, sparking new ideas and understanding

The grants are targeted toward research that directly impacts policy, governance, and social development, thereby ensuring that academic insights translate into meaningful, actionable outcomes for communities.

Expected Deliverables for Grant Recipients

Recipients of the Doctoral Research Grant are expected to deliver a variety of outputs designed to communicate research findings to both academic and public audiences:

  1. Final Report – A concise project summary of up to 1,500 words highlighting key findings, outcomes, and policy implications.

  2. Policy Brief – A summary emphasizing key results, recommendations, and implications for local or national policymaking.

  3. Research Majlis Presentation – A public or hybrid session hosted by the Foundation to present findings, engage stakeholders, and discuss future steps.

  4. Policy Paper or Op-Ed – A scholarly, policy-oriented publication analyzing findings and presenting actionable recommendations.

  5. Research Paper – A detailed article suitable for submission to a peer-reviewed academic journal.

Financial Support and Coverage

The Doctoral Research Grant provides full financial support to ensure scholars can focus entirely on their research, including:

  • Return airfare to the UAE

  • Furnished accommodation for up to 12 months

  • Stipend for living costs

  • Research-related expenses and support

This funding model allows recipients to conduct field research without financial barriers, facilitating thorough and in-depth study.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Doctoral Research Grant, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be a current PhD student at an accredited university in good academic standing

  • Have a minimum TOEFL iBT score of 85 or IELTS score of 6.5 if English is not the first language

  • Have completed all PhD coursework and be ready to conduct the field research phase

  • Possess a research proposal approved (or in the process of approval) by the applicant’s advisor and graduate program, including a literature review and detailed methodology

  • Obtain Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval from the home institution for the research

  • Include Ras Al Khaimah or the UAE as part of the research site

  • Applicants cannot pursue research in business, marketing, engineering, IT, computer science, lab-based sciences, or medical degrees, as these are considered outside the grant’s policy-oriented focus

Preference is given to doctoral research in areas such as education, public policy, urban planning, economics, public health, and environmental studies.

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

  • Academic merit and performance

  • Relevance and innovation of proposed research

  • Alignment with the Al Qasimi Foundation’s mission and policy goals

  • Potential impact on the Ras Al Khaimah community and UAE policy

  • Strength of professional and academic support from referees

Only two Doctoral Research Grants are awarded annually, making the selection process highly competitive. Proposals are chosen for their ability to produce policy-relevant, innovative, and impactful research.

Application Process

The 2026 application cycle will reopen in December 2025. Prospective applicants must:

  1. Create an account on the Al Qasimi Foundation’s grant management portal

  2. Complete the online application form

  3. Upload all required documents, including:

    • Two-page essay/cover letter detailing interest, relevant experience, alignment with the Foundation’s mission, and long-term goals

    • CV or resume outlining academic and professional accomplishments

    • Current doctoral transcript (and transcripts for all previous degrees)

    • Official TOEFL or IELTS score (if English is a secondary language)

    • Approved research proposal, including any special equipment or facilities needed

    • Completed Doctoral Research Grant Budget Template

    • IRB approval documentation (or planned approval)

    • Two letters of reference, including one from the doctoral advisor

All submissions become the property of the Sheikh Saud bin Saqr Al Qasimi Foundation for Policy Research and will not be returned.

Evaluation and Timeline

  • Application Deadline: April 1, 2026 (23:59 GST)

  • Review Period: Approximately 8–10 weeks, including administrative screening, external peer review, and final management approval

  • Notification of Results: Late May 2026

  • Project Start Dates: Between June and September 2026

The Foundation encourages applicants to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid technical or administrative issues.

Why Apply?

The Doctoral Research Grant is a prestigious and highly competitive opportunity that allows doctoral candidates to:

  • Conduct policy-relevant, applied research in the UAE

  • Gain professional recognition and expand research networks

  • Produce outputs that can influence local and national policy

  • Experience cultural exchange and contribute to regional research capacity

For doctoral scholars committed to making a meaningful impact through research, this grant provides the resources, mentorship, and platform needed to achieve academic and societal influence.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Al Qasimi Foundation Doctoral Research Grants 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in the UAE appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Environmental Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Report from the GEF Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN), a flagship initiative of Internews, has launched an exceptional opportunity for journalists from low- and middle-income countries to participate in a fully funded fellowship covering the Eighth Global Environment Facility (GEF) Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. This unique program is designed to empower journalists to deepen their expertise in environmental reporting […]

The post Global Environmental Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Report from the GEF Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN), a flagship initiative of Internews, has launched an exceptional opportunity for journalists from low- and middle-income countries to participate in a fully funded fellowship covering the Eighth Global Environment Facility (GEF) Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan. This unique program is designed to empower journalists to deepen their expertise in environmental reporting while covering one of the world’s most influential global environmental governance meetings.

Selected fellows will travel to Uzbekistan to attend and report from the Assembly taking place between May 31 and June 6, 2026, where key decisions shaping global climate and environmental financing will be discussed. The fellowship will not only provide journalists with access to global environmental leaders and policymakers but also equip them with the training and mentorship necessary to report on complex environmental issues that affect millions worldwide.

The application deadline for this fellowship is March 31, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific/Niue time).

Understanding the Global Environment Facility (GEF)

The Global Environment Facility (GEF) is a major international financial mechanism that supports environmental initiatives across the world. Since its establishment in 1991, the GEF has played a critical role in funding projects aimed at addressing the most pressing environmental challenges.

Over the past three decades, the GEF has provided more than $27 billion in funding, primarily through grants, and has mobilized an additional $155 billion in co-financing for environmental programs worldwide.

The facility works closely with global environmental agreements, including:

  • The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD)

  • The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)

  • The Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants (POPs)

  • The United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD)

  • The Minamata Convention on Mercury

Through these partnerships, the GEF supports initiatives that protect biodiversity, improve climate resilience, strengthen food security, protect water resources, and promote sustainable development.

In addition to the GEF Trust Fund, several specialized funding mechanisms operate under the broader GEF umbrella, including:

  • The Least Developed Countries Fund (LDCF)

  • The Special Climate Change Fund (SCCF)

  • The Global Biodiversity Framework Fund (GBFF)

  • The Nagoya Protocol Implementation Fund (NPIF)

  • The Capacity-building Initiative for Transparency Trust Fund (CBIT)

These funds support critical environmental programs that influence agriculture, health systems, infrastructure development, disaster risk reduction, and ecosystem protection across vulnerable regions.

What is the GEF Assembly?

The GEF Assembly serves as the governing body of the Global Environment Facility and convenes representatives from all 186 member countries. It is responsible for reviewing and approving major policies, operational strategies, and institutional decisions related to the global environmental financing system.

The Eighth GEF Assembly in Samarkand will gather more than 1,000 participants, including:

  • Government ministers

  • International environmental experts

  • Civil society representatives

  • Indigenous leaders

  • Business and private sector leaders

  • Representatives of GEF partner agencies

  • Policymakers shaping global climate strategies

Throughout the week-long event, participants will deliberate on issues such as environmental financing, climate adaptation strategies, biodiversity protection, and sustainable development.

The Assembly program includes:

  • Nine high-level policy roundtables

  • A Civil Society Forum scheduled for June 3

  • Multiple thematic side events

  • Field visits to environmental project sites

  • Partnership and networking sessions

These discussions are expected to produce important outcomes that influence global environmental governance and funding priorities.

What the Fellowship Offers

Through this fellowship, five journalists will be selected to report directly from the Assembly in Samarkand. Fellows will receive extensive support and training to ensure they can effectively cover the complex environmental negotiations and policy discussions taking place during the event.

Pre-conference preparation

Before traveling to Uzbekistan, fellows will receive access to resources, tools, and preparatory materials from EJN. These resources are designed to help journalists understand the key environmental issues that will be debated during the Assembly and prepare them to develop impactful story ideas.

On-site training and mentorship

While attending the Assembly, fellows will participate in a structured program that includes:

  • Orientation sessions on the major environmental issues under discussion

  • Daily briefings to contextualize negotiations and policy developments

  • Opportunities to interview high-level policymakers and experts

  • Mentorship from an experienced senior journalist affiliated with EJN

This mentor will provide guidance on story development, reporting strategies, and analysis of the Assembly’s outcomes. Fellows will also receive feedback and editorial support to help refine their reporting.

Collaborative learning environment

The fellowship cohort will consist of journalists from different regions and media formats. Fellows will exchange ideas, share reporting strategies, and collaborate on covering environmental issues from diverse global perspectives.

Publishing and Reporting Requirements

Journalists participating in the fellowship may produce stories in a wide range of formats, including:

  • Print journalism

  • Online reporting

  • Radio broadcasts

  • Television or video reporting

Stories may be published in any language, allowing journalists to reach audiences in their home countries effectively.

However, fellows must provide:

  • English summaries of their stories

  • At least one full story translated into English for wider dissemination

This requirement ensures that the insights generated during the fellowship can be shared globally.

Editorial Independence and Attribution

The Earth Journalism Network fully respects the editorial independence of participating journalists. Fellows are free to report on the Assembly’s developments according to their professional judgment.

However, all stories produced through the fellowship must include the following acknowledgement:

“This story was produced as part of a reporting fellowship to the Eighth GEF Assembly supported by Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.”

Fellows may publish their work first through their affiliated media organizations. At the same time, EJN and its partners retain rights to edit, republish, broadcast, and distribute the stories.

Fellowship Funding and Coverage

The fellowship is fully funded, ensuring that selected journalists can participate without financial barriers.

The program covers:

  • Economy-class international airfare

  • Accommodation in Samarkand

  • Meals during the event

  • Travel insurance

  • Local transportation costs

  • Possible rail transportation between Tashkent and Samarkand

  • Press accreditation support

  • Logistical assistance during the conference

Participants may also receive financial support for transportation expenses during transit.

Visa application processes remain the responsibility of the fellows, though visa fees can be reimbursed.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet several eligibility criteria to be considered for the fellowship.

Eligible candidates must:

  • Be professional journalists working with or representing an established media outlet in a low- or middle-income country

  • Demonstrate prior experience reporting on environmental issues

  • Have a strong command of English

  • Be able to attend the entire Assembly in Samarkand from May 31 to June 6, 2026

  • Provide a letter of support from an editor or supervisor confirming publication of fellowship-related reporting

  • Submit samples of previous journalistic work related to environmental topics

Work samples may be submitted as documents or links and may be in any language, provided they include an English synopsis.

While journalists of any age may apply, preference will be given to applicants aged 35 and under.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several factors, including:

  • Demonstrated experience in environmental and climate reporting

  • A well-developed reporting plan outlining story ideas from the Assembly

  • Potential audience reach of the applicant’s media outlet

  • A strong professional relationship with a reputable media organization

  • Geographic and media diversity among the selected fellows

Applicants who have engaged in unethical or improper professional conduct may be disqualified from consideration.

Application Process

Journalists interested in this fellowship must apply through the official Earth Journalism Network application platform.

Applicants will need to create or log into an account, complete the application form, and upload all required documents.

Required materials include:

  • A reporting proposal outlining story ideas

  • A letter of support from a media outlet editor or supervisor

  • Samples of relevant journalistic work

  • Supporting documentation requested during the application process

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications at least one day before the deadline to avoid technical issues.

Key Dates

Application Deadline: March 31, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific/Niue Time)
Fellowship Event Dates: May 31 – June 6, 2026

Applicants will be notified of the selection results by mid-April 2026.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Environmental issues are among the most urgent global challenges of the 21st century. Climate change, biodiversity loss, pollution, and ecosystem degradation disproportionately affect communities in low- and middle-income countries.

By empowering journalists from these regions to report directly from high-level environmental negotiations, this fellowship helps ensure that global audiences receive accurate, contextualized, and impactful reporting on decisions that shape the future of the planet.

For journalists passionate about environmental storytelling and global climate governance, this fellowship represents an invaluable opportunity to expand their professional skills, strengthen international networks, and contribute to informed public dialogue on environmental policy.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Global Environmental Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Report from the GEF Assembly in Samarkand, Uzbekistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) is inviting applications for the 2026 Institute Advisory Board (IAB) Membership Cycle, offering professionals, researchers, and community leaders an opportunity to influence the future of health research in Canada. These advisory boards play a critical role in guiding CIHR’s Institutes by providing expert advice, strategic perspectives, and community […]

The post Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) is inviting applications for the 2026 Institute Advisory Board (IAB) Membership Cycle, offering professionals, researchers, and community leaders an opportunity to influence the future of health research in Canada.

These advisory boards play a critical role in guiding CIHR’s Institutes by providing expert advice, strategic perspectives, and community insight to strengthen Canada’s health research ecosystem.

Serving as an IAB member allows individuals to contribute to national health research priorities, collaborate with experts across disciplines, and ensure research initiatives reflect the needs of diverse communities.

Application Deadline: April 25, 2026

What Are CIHR Institute Advisory Boards?

Each of CIHR’s 13 Institutes has its own Institute Advisory Board composed of 8–14 volunteer members from Canada and abroad. These members provide advice to the Institute’s Scientific Director on a wide range of activities, including:

  • Research priorities and strategic planning

  • Knowledge mobilization strategies

  • Community engagement initiatives

  • Cross-Institute collaborations

  • Health policy and innovation

The boards represent a broad spectrum of expertise across the health research ecosystem, including academia, government, healthcare institutions, industry, and community organizations.

The Role of an Institute Advisory Board Member

IAB members serve in an independent advisory capacity, providing insights and recommendations that strengthen the direction and impact of health research initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Members contribute by:

  • Advising on Institute initiatives and strategic research priorities

  • Encouraging interdisciplinary health research collaborations

  • Strengthening partnerships with stakeholders and communities

  • Supporting knowledge translation and research impact

  • Considering ethical, policy, diversity, and inclusion factors in research initiatives

Members also help ensure research priorities address emerging health challenges and the needs of diverse Canadian populations.

Leadership and Governance

Each Institute Advisory Board operates with a leadership structure that includes either:

  • A Chair and Vice-Chair, or

  • Two Co-Chairs

These leaders guide discussions, develop meeting agendas with the Scientific Director, and ensure that advice represents the collective views of the board.

Appointments are made by CIHR’s Governing Council based on expertise, experience, and representation needs.

Membership Term and Time Commitment

Selected members are invited to serve for:

  • Initial term: Up to 3 years

  • Possible renewal: Up to an additional 3 years

  • Maximum service: 6 years

Board meetings occur at least twice per year, either virtually or in person.

While membership is voluntary, CIHR reimburses travel and meeting-related expenses at standard Government of Canada rates.

Areas of Expertise Sought for 2026

For the 2026 cycle, CIHR Institutes are seeking applicants with expertise in areas such as:

  • Artificial intelligence and data science in health research

  • Indigenous health research and systems

  • Health policy development and implementation

  • Clinical research and trials

  • Precision medicine and oncology

  • Mental health and substance use policy

  • Nutrition and metabolic diseases

  • Population health and public health policy

  • Digital health and learning health systems

  • Gerontechnology and aging research

Many Institutes are also seeking representation from individuals with lived experience, government professionals, patient partners, and international experts.

Why Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board?

Serving on an IAB offers a unique opportunity to influence national health research priorities while working alongside leaders from multiple sectors.

Impact Canada’s Health Research Agenda

Your insights can help shape policies and research strategies that improve health outcomes across the country.

Expand Your Professional Network

Collaborate with researchers, policymakers, clinicians, and community leaders from Canada and around the world.

Represent Diverse Voices

CIHR is committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion. Advisory board members help ensure health research reflects the needs of all Canadians.

Commitment to Equity, Diversity and Inclusion

CIHR actively encourages applications from individuals who bring diverse perspectives to the advisory boards.

This includes candidates who identify as Indigenous, Francophone, gender diverse, living with disabilities, or members of racialized and marginalized communities.

Representation across regions, sectors, and lived experiences is considered an essential part of building inclusive health research leadership.

How the Selection Process Works

Applications are reviewed during the annual membership cycle. Scientific Directors evaluate candidates based on:

  • Expertise and professional experience

  • Gaps in board representation

  • Diversity and regional representation

  • Current vacancies on each Institute’s board

Selected candidates help fill expertise or representation needs within specific Institutes.

Applications remain active in the IAB candidate pool for three years, allowing applicants to be considered for future openings.

Apply for the 2026 CIHR IAB Membership Cycle

Professionals interested in shaping the future of health research in Canada are encouraged to submit an application before the deadline.

APPLY HERE. 

Application Deadline: April 25, 2026

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

Learn more and apply through the official CIHR Institute Advisory Boards page.

The post Help Shape Canada’s Health Research Future: Apply to Join a CIHR Institute Advisory Board appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
